engineCodes.h2: Mazda Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Mazda engine — SKYACTIV-G, SKYACTIV-D and MZR. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click “Get Quote” on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Mazda specialists.

groups.name: SKYACTIV-G Petrol
groups.failureNote: SKYACTIV-G petrol engines are generally reliable but suffer carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection, particularly the 2.0L variant in Mazda3 and CX-30 . Cylinder head cracking (early 2.5L) and thermostat failures also occur. Our rebuilt units include professionally cleaned intake ports and new valve stem seals.

groups.name: SKYACTIV-D Diesel
groups.failureNote: SKYACTIV-D engines are known for severe intake carbon buildup (coked intake), DPF clogging, and P2002 DPF efficiency codes leading to engine failure. Excessive oil consumption and EGR issues are also common . Our rebuilt engines include professionally cleaned intakes, new EGR valves, and upgraded oil control components.

groups.name: MZR Petrol
groups.failureNote: MZR petrol engines suffer timing chain stretch and VVT actuator failure, causing rattle on startup (particularly the 2.5L) . If ignored, this leads to valve damage. Our rebuilt units include new timing chains, upgraded tensioners, and refurbished VVT units.

groups.name: MZR-CD Diesel
groups.failureNote: *Common failure points (expandable note): MZR-CD engines suffer catastrophic carbon buildup, DPF clogging, and fuel injector failure. Knocking noises are common . Our rebuilt engines include professionally cleaned intakes, new injectors, and refurbished DPF systems.

groups.name: Legacy & Classic Engines
groups.failureNote: *` button should pre-fill the engine code in the quote request form. ## Keyword Coverage Table

commonProblems.h2: Common Mazda Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Mazda’s reputation balances affordable motoring with genuine driver engagement, but specific engine families—particularly the 2.2-litre Skyactiv-D diesel and earlier MZR petrol units—have developed known failure points that drive the majority of replacement searches in the UK. Every factory-remanufactured or specialist-rebuilt Mazda engine supplied through the EngineMarket network includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original design weak points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the four most common failure clusters, what they cost to fix at a dealer versus a specialist, and when replacement becomes the only sensible option.

problems.h4: Skyactiv-D Carbon Buildup – The “Coked Intake” Crisis That Kills Mazda Diesels
problems.affectedModels: Mazda CX-5 (2012–2017, SH & S8), Mazda 6 (2012–2018, SH & S8), Mazda 3 (2014–2019, S8), Mazda CX-7 (2012–2015, S8) – Mazda 2.2-litre Skyactiv-D (SH, S8, S9 series)
problems.rootCause: Excessive exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) combined with the absence of port fuel injection allows soot and oil vapour to bake onto intake valves and manifolds, progressively choking airflow. The result is blocked DPFs, repeated regeneration failures, and the infamous P2002 DPF efficiency code.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Chemical or media-blast decarbonisation of inlet manifold, EGR valve, and throttle body; forced DPF regen; software reset | 20,000–40,000 miles if driving habits remain unchanged.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable only before permanent DPF damage occurs. |

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – Full Intake System + DPF Clean |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | As above plus professional DPF off-car thermal clean, new DPF pressure sensors, and updated engine management calibration | 40,000–60,000 miles. Recommended for cars already showing P2002 codes but without turbo or injector damage. |

| Supply & fit of fully rebuilt SH/S8 engine with redesigned EGR cooler, uprated injector seals, and bench-tested cylinder head; 12-month warranty | 80,000+ miles with better carbon resistance than factory original. Necessary when valve damage or turbo failure has occurred. |

problems.recommendation: If your CX-5 or Mazda 6 has only started logging DPF efficiency codes and hasn’t entered limp mode, the intermediate-tier decoke plus professional DPF clean offers the best value. Once the turbo begins over-speeding or injector spray patterns degrade (typically after repeated blocked DPF events), only a fully rebuilt engine with the updated EGR and injector hardware provides long-term reliability. On a vehicle worth £4,000–£7,000, invest in the specialist intermediate fix early; beyond that threshold, compare rebuilt engine prices.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt Mazda SH and S8 Skyactiv-D engine prices for your CX-5 or Mazda 6

problems.h4: MZR-CD Diesel Injector & Carbon Catastrophe – When a Service Item Becomes Terminal
problems.affectedModels: Mazda 6 (2005–2012, R2AA, R2BA), Mazda 3 (2006–2013, R2AA), Mazda CX-7 (2007–2012, R2BA) – Mazda 2.0 & 2.2 MZR-CD
problems.rootCause: The Siemens fuel injectors on early MZR-CD units develop internal leakage, causing excessive fuel dilution of engine oil. Combined with the high EGR rates of the era, intake carbon buildup becomes so severe that valves fail to seat, leading to compression loss and, ultimately, bore polishing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace all four injectors with refurbished or new OEM units; decarbonise intake manifold and EGR; oil and filter change | 40,000–60,000 miles.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if compression is still within spec (no misfire on cold start). |

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply & fit of fully remanufactured R2AA or R2BA engine with uprated injector seals, cleaned intake ports, new timing chain kit, and 12-month warranty | 80,000+ miles. Necessary if bore compression has dropped below 18 bar or if the engine hydro-locks due to diesel dilution of oil. |

problems.recommendation: This engine’s fatal flaw is that injector leakage often goes unnoticed until oil dilution destroys the bottom end. We recommend annual oil analysis for any MZR-CD past 70,000 miles. If caught early (oil level rising, diesel smell on dipstick), specialist injector replacement and a full decarbonisation saves the engine. Once you hear bottom-end knock or fail a compression test, do not throw money at repairs—move straight to a rebuilt unit with the updated injector hardware. On a 2008–2012 Mazda 6 worth £2,500–£4,000, replacement is the correct financial decision.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt R2AA and R2BA MZR-CD engines with upgraded injector sealing

problems.h4: MZR Petrol Timing Chain Stretch – The Rattle That Leads to Valve Disaster
problems.rootCause: The original timing chain tensioner relies on oil pressure and a relatively weak spring. Extended oil change intervals or low oil levels cause the tensioner to retract, allowing chain slap against the timing cover. Once the chain stretches beyond the tensioner’s travel range, it jumps timing, and valves meet pistons.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace timing chain, guides, tensioner (updated design), and camshaft sprocket bolts; new front crank seal | 80,000–100,000 miles.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable at first sign of cold-start rattle before any misfire or cam correlation codes appear. |

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Rebuilt MZR Petrol Engine |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply & fit of fully rebuilt LF17/L813/L3-VE engine with new timing assembly, uprated oil pump, and bench-tested cylinder head; 12-month warranty | 100,000+ miles. Required if chain has jumped and caused valve contact (confirmed by compression loss or bore-scope). |

problems.recommendation: The early warning sign is a 1-2 second rattle on cold start from the timing cover area. If you hear it, book the preventative chain and tensioner replacement immediately—it costs roughly the same as a clutch replacement and saves the engine. Once the engine has jumped time (typically during cranking or under load), valves are almost certainly bent, and a rebuilt engine with a fresh cylinder head is the only path. On a Mazda 3 or 6 valued at £2,000–£4,500, the minor fix pays for itself if the rest of the car is solid.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt MZR LF17 and L813 petrol engine prices for your Mazda 3 or MX-5

problems.h4: Skyactiv Direct Injection Carbon – The Petrol Problem Mazda Didn’t Solve
problems.affectedModels: Mazda 3 (2014–2019, PE-VPS), Mazda 6 (2013–2018, PE-VPS), Mazda CX-5 (2013–2017, PE-VPS), Mazda CX-3 (2015–2020, P5) – Skyactiv-G 2.0-litre (PE-VPS) and 1.5-litre (P5)
problems.rootCause: As a direct-injection petrol engine, fuel never washes over the intake valves. Crankcase vapour and EGR soot bake onto valve stems and heads, progressively reducing airflow. Unlike BMW’s N43/N53, Mazda did not fit a secondary port injector, so manual decarbonisation is the only fix.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove intake manifold; walnut-shell blast intake ports and valve heads; clean throttle body and EGR; software adaptation reset | 30,000–50,000 miles.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable as preventative maintenance or at first signs of cold misfire or reduced MPG. |

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Walnut blast decarbonisation plus professional ultrasonic cleaning of all four direct injectors; new injector seals and decoupler elements | 50,000–70,000 miles. Required if injector spray has been compromised (confirmed by fuel trim deviations). |

| Supply & fit of fully rebuilt PE-VPS or P5 engine with professionally cleaned head, new valve stem seals, and bench-tested injectors; 12-month warranty | 80,000+ miles. Only necessary if carbon buildup has caused valve burning or ring sticking (excessive oil consumption). |

problems.recommendation: On a high-mileage Mazda 3 or CX-5, the minor decarbonisation every 40,000 miles is the single best investment you can make. Leaving carbon buildup leads to cold-start misfires, failed MOT emissions, and eventually burnt valves. For most owners, the specialist minor package at £250–£400 is the sweet spot. Replacement is rarely needed unless the car has been neglected past 120,000 miles without any cleaning. Compare specialist prices versus dealer—we regularly see £300+ saved on the same work.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for Skyactiv-G decarbonisation or compare rebuilt PE-VPS engine prices

problems.h4: MX-5 Enthusiast Failures – Crankshaft Keyway Wear (NA/NB) & Timing Chain Rattle (NC-ND)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Keyway wear: 60,000–120,000 miles (NA/NB). Chain rattle: 80,000–110,000 miles (NC/ND).
problems.rootCause: On NA/NB 1.6 and 1.8 engines, an underspecified crankshaft nose and keyway design allows the pulley boss to oscillate, gradually hammering the keyway wider until timing slips. On NC/ND MZR and Skyactiv-G engines, the same timing chain tensioner weakness as mainstream MZR engines causes cold-start rattle and eventual chain jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace timing chain, guides, tensioner (updated ND-spec part), camshaft sprockets, and front crank seal | 80,000–100,000 miles. Required at first sign of persistent chain rattle on NC/ND. |

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Rebuilt MX-5 Engine (Any Gen) |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply & fit of fully rebuilt BP, LF17, or PE-VPS engine with crankshaft keyway reinforcement (NA/NB) or updated timing assembly (NC/ND); 12-month warranty | 100,000+ miles. Only necessary for NA/NB with terminal keyway damage or NC/ND that has jumped time. |

problems.recommendation: For NA/NB owners, the specialist Loctite fix is a proven long-term repair for road cars if caught before the crank sprocket can wobble. If the keyway has already allowed the timing belt to jump, the crank nose is usually scrap—go straight to a rebuilt engine with a new or welded crank. For NC/ND owners, treat chain rattle as urgent: £600–£900 for a specialist chain replacement saves a £2,500–£3,800 engine. On an MX-5, the enthusiast specialist network delivers better results than main dealers for every single repair tier above.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt BP, LF17, and PE-VPS MX-5 engine prices – specialist rebuilds with warranty

finalCta.h4: Don’t Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mazda
finalCta.paragraph: Choosing between a costly main dealer repair, an independent specialist fix, or a fully rebuilt engine comes down to the specific failure, your car’s value, and how long you plan to keep it. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare quotes from trusted UK Mazda specialists—whether you need a decarbonisation, a timing chain replacement, or a fully rebuilt Skyactiv-D or MZR engine with upgraded components. Every engine supplied through our network carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, and we compare both supply-only and supply-and-fit options nationwide.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mazda Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mazda model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Mazda Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Mazda engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle’s age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Mazda Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM-spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,500 – £3,800 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Mazda Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as uprated timing chains (MZR), reinforced oil control rings (SKYACTIV-D), and improved side seals (Renesis rotary) – for a longer service life than a standard replacement.

types.title: Used Mazda Engines
types.description: A tested, low-mileage take-out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available.

types.title: Mazda Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory-spec, as-new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models like the MX-5 or RX-8, or owners wanting a zero-mile engine.

types.title: Mazda Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn’t had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget-sensitive models like the Mazda2, Mazda3 (BK), or 323 where a full recon isn’t economical.

types.title: Mazda Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Mazda specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £400 – £800 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Mazda engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12-month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Mazda model today.

engineSizes.h2: Mazda Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Mazda engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Mazda 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre diesel appeared primarily in the Mazda2 (DJ series) as a high-efficiency option for urban and commuter use. Part of the Skyactiv-D family, this engine code N5 offered strong economy but suffered from the same carbon buildup issues as larger Skyactiv-D units due to EGR clogging and low-speed driving habits that prevented proper DPF regeneration
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mazda 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: Mazda used the PSA-derived 1.6-litre HDi diesel (DV6) in several models during the Ford-era partnership. This engine delivers modest power but good fuel economy, though it carries the well-documented weaknesses of the DV6 family: turbo actuator seizure, EGR valve clogging, and injector seal leaks. While not as failure-prone as Mazda's own 2.2-litre diesels, high-mileage examples still regularly appear in replacement searches
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Mazda 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre MZR-CD diesel (engine code R2AA) powered early Mazda6 and Mazda3 models before being largely replaced by the 2.2-litre Skyactiv-D. This engine shares the Siemens injector and carbon buildup issues of its larger MZR-CD sibling, with fuel dilution of engine oil being the primary killer. Buyers searching for "Mazda 2.0 diesel engine" are typically looking to replace a failed R2AA unit rather than repair it, as bore polishing and low compression are common by 100,000 miles
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mazda 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2-litre diesel is the single largest engine replacement segment for Mazda in the UK. Two distinct families exist: the earlier MZR-CD (R2BA, R2CA) and the later Skyactiv-D (SH, S8, S9). Both suffer carbon-related failures, but the Skyactiv-D’s intake coking crisis—triggering P2002 DPF efficiency codes—drives the majority of searches. Owners routinely look for "Mazda 2.2 diesel engine problems" and "Mazda CX-5 DPF regeneration failed", with most concluding that a rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR and injector hardware is the only durable fix
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Mazda 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3-litre MZR petrol engine (ZJ, ZY) appeared in the Mazda2 and early Mazda3 as an entry-level option. It is a relatively robust unit but suffers from timing chain stretch at higher mileages, particularly when oil changes were extended. The 13B-MSP Renesis rotary engine (1.3-litre nominal displacement, though technically a 1.3-litre twin-rotor) powered the RX-8 and is a completely different proposition—sought primarily for rebuilds due to side-seal wear and low compression
items.cta: Compare Mazda 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: Ford’s ZJ-series 1.4-litre engine (also badged as Mazda ZJ) featured in early Mazda2 and Mazda3 models as the base petrol option. It is generally reliable but underpowered for the Mazda3, and timing chain stretch becomes an issue on poorly maintained examples. Replacement demand is relatively low compared to larger Mazda engines, but supply-and-fit queries do appear for high-mileage city cars
items.cta: Compare Mazda 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre Skyactiv-G (P5) replaced the older 1.4 and 1.6 units in the Mazda2 and Mazda3. It is a direct-injection petrol engine, meaning intake valve carbon buildup is the primary long-term concern. Owners searching for "Mazda 1.5 engine replacement" are typically dealing with cold-start misfires, reduced fuel economy, and failed MOT emissions due to carbon-clogged valves. A walnut blast decarbonisation every 40,000 miles is the recommended preventative measure
items.cta: Compare Mazda 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The Ford-sourced 1.6-litre ZJ engine (also known as the Mazda ZJ) was widely used across Mazda’s Ford-era lineup. It is a durable unit compared to Mazda’s diesels, but high-mileage examples suffer from timing chain stretch and VVT actuator failure, producing a pronounced rattle on cold start. Replacement engines are frequently sought for Mazda3 and Mazda5 models where the chain has jumped, bending valves
items.cta: Compare Mazda 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8-litre MZR (L813) and BP-series engines cover both mainstream and performance applications. The BP 1.8 in early MX-5s (NA/NB) is a robust unit prone to crankshaft keyway wear on the 1.8 as well as the 1.6. The later L813 in front-wheel-drive Mazda3 and Mazda5 suffers from the same timing chain and VVT issues as other MZR petrols. Searches for "Mazda 1.8 engine replacement" split between MX-5 enthusiasts looking for BP rebuilds and mainstream owners dealing with chain stretch
items.cta: Compare Mazda 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre petrol is one of Mazda’s most common displacements, spanning three distinct families: the MZR LF17 (2003–2013), the Skyactiv-G PE-VPS (2012–present), and the Skyactiv-X (2019–present). The LF17 suffers timing chain stretch and VVT rattle. The PE-VPS (direct injection) is generally reliable but requires periodic intake valve cleaning. The Skyactiv-X uses spark-controlled compression ignition and is too new to have developed a widespread failure profile, though early units are now entering the used engine market. Replacement demand is highest for the LF17, followed by decarbonisation quotes for the PE-VPS
items.cta: Compare Mazda 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda 2.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.3-litre MZR (L3-VE, L3-VDT) appeared in mainstream Mazda6 and CX-7 models, and in turbocharged form in the Mazda3 MPS and Mazda6 MPS. The normally aspirated L3-VE suffers the same timing chain and VVT issues as smaller MZRs. The turbocharged L3-VDT (also known as the DISI Turbo) adds high-pressure fuel pump failures (leading to lean running and piston damage) and carbon buildup on intake valves. Enthusiast searches for "Mazda 2.3 turbo engine" or "Mazda 2.3 engine replacement" are common, with many opting for rebuilt units with upgraded internals
items.cta: Compare Mazda 2.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5-litre Skyactiv-G (PY-VPS) powers larger Mazda models including the CX-5, Mazda6, and CX-9 (import). It is a further development of the 2.0-litre Skyactiv-G, sharing the direct-injection architecture and therefore the same carbon buildup vulnerability. Owners search for "Mazda 2.5 engine replacement" less frequently than the 2.0-litre, but the failure profile is identical: intake valve coking leading to cold misfires and reduced performance. The naturally aspirated 2.5-litre is also a popular engine swap for MX-5 NC owners seeking more torque
items.cta: Compare Mazda 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda 2.5L Turbo Petrol Engines
items.description: The turbocharged version of the 2.5-litre Skyactiv-G (PY-VPTS) appears in higher-performance Mazda variants such as the Mazda3 MPS successor (Mazda3 Turbo) and Mazda6 Turbo, along with the CX-5 and CX-9. While still relatively new in the UK aftermarket, early failure reports point to cracked cylinder heads (casting issues on some production batches) and oil consumption due to turbo seal wear. Enthusiast searches for “Mazda 2.5 turbo engine replacement” are growing as these vehicles enter higher-mileage territory
items.cta: Compare Mazda 2.5L turbo petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda Electric Motors
items.description: Mazda’s only fully electric vehicle (BEV) in the UK market is the MX-30, which uses a 35.5 kWh battery and a single AC synchronous electric motor. Unlike combustion engines, electric motors are far less prone to mechanical failure, but battery degradation and cooling system faults are the primary concerns as these vehicles age. Replacement searches for “Mazda electric motor” are extremely rare; when powertrain issues occur, battery module or inverter faults are far more common than motor failure itself. Motor code(s): e-Skyactiv (AC synchronous)
items.cta: Enquire about Mazda electric motor repair

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Mazda Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Mazda engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Mazda specialists.

entries.title: SH / S8 / S9 2.2L Diesel
entries.description: The 2.2L SKYACTIV-D (SH – 148bhp, S8 – 173bhp, S9 – 173bhp) powered the 2012-2020 Mazda6 (GJ/GL), 2012-2020 CX-5 (KE/KF), and 2014-2019 Mazda3 (BM/BN). Severe intake carbon buildup ("coked intake"), DPF clogging, P2002 efficiency codes, and valve spring failure are the primary replacement drivers. Our rebuilt units include professionally cleaned intakes, new EGR valves, upgraded oil control rings, and bench testing.
entries.cta: Get quotes for SH, S8, S9 Mazda engine replacement

entries.title: S5 1.5L Diesel
entries.description: The 1.5L SKYACTIV-D (S5, 103bhp) featured in the 2014-2022 Mazda2 (DJ) and 2017-2019 Mazda3 (BM). Carbon buildup and EGR valve sticking are the most common failure points. Rebuilt units include intake decarbonisation, new injector seals, and a full gasket set.
entries.cta: Compare S5 1.5L Mazda diesel engine prices

entries.title: LF17 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: The 2.0L MZR (LF17, 147bhp) powered the 2003-2009 Mazda3 (BK), 2002-2008 Mazda6 (GG), and 2005-2015 MX-5 (NC). Timing chain stretch and VVT actuator failure cause cold-start rattle and eventual valve damage if ignored. Our rebuilt units include a new timing chain kit, upgraded tensioner, refurbished VVT unit, and water pump.
entries.cta: Get quotes for LF17 Mazda engine replacement

entries.title: L3-VE 2.3L Petrol
entries.description: The 2.3L MZR (L3-VE, 158bhp) appeared in the 2002-2008 Mazda6 (GG), 2005-2010 Mazda5 (CR), and 2006-2012 Mazda CX-7 (ER). Timing chain stretch, VVT actuator wear, and oil consumption due to stuck piston rings are common. Our rebuilt units include new timing components, revised piston rings, and a full gasket set.
entries.cta: Compare L3-VE 2.3L Mazda petrol engine prices

entries.title: L3-VDT 2.3L Turbo Petrol
entries.description: (MPS) The turbocharged L3-VDT (MPS, 256bhp) powered the 2006-2013 Mazda3 MPS (BK/BL) and 2005-2012 Mazda6 MPS (GG). Turbocharger failure, timing chain stretch, and cracked cylinder heads are the dominant replacement triggers. Our rebuilt units include an uprated timing chain kit, turbocharger inspection/replacement, and a pressure-tested cylinder head.
entries.cta: Get quotes for L3-VDT MPS Mazda engine replacement

entries.title: L5-VE 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: The 2.5L MZR (L5-VE, 170bhp) featured in the 2009-2013 Mazda6 (GH) and 2010-2013 Mazda3 (BL). Timing chain stretch and VVT actuator failure are the primary issues, often presenting as a rattle on startup. Rebuilt units include a new timing chain kit, upgraded tensioner, and refurbished VVT actuator.
entries.cta: Compare L5-VE 2.5L Mazda petrol engine prices

entries.title: L813 1.8L Petrol
entries.description: The 1.8L MZR (L813, 118bhp) powered the 2003-2009 Mazda3 (BK), 2005-2010 Mazda5 (CR), and 2002-2008 Mazda6 (GG). Timing chain stretch and VVT actuator failure are the most common replacement drivers. Our rebuilt units include new timing components, refurbished VVT, and a full service kit.
entries.cta: Get quotes for L813 1.8L Mazda engine replacement

entries.title: R2AA / R2BA 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: The 2.0L MZR-CD (R2AA – 138bhp, R2BA – 141bhp) powered the 2002-2008 Mazda6 (GG/GH) and 2005-2010 Mazda MPV (LY). Carbon buildup, DPF clogging, and fuel injector failure are the dominant issues. Our rebuilt units include professionally cleaned intakes, new injectors, a refurbished DPF, and a new timing chain.
entries.cta: Get quotes for R2AA, R2BA Mazda diesel engine replacement

entries.title: R2CA 2.2L Diesel
entries.description: The 2.2L MZR-CD (R2CA, 158bhp) appeared in the 2008-2012 Mazda6 (GH) and 2007-2012 Mazda CX-7 (ER). Carbon buildup, DPF clogging, and injector failure are common, often accompanied by knocking noises. Our rebuilt units include intake decarbonisation, new injectors, and a full gasket and seal set.
entries.cta: Compare R2CA 2.2L Mazda diesel engine prices

entries.title: PE-VPS 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: The 2.0L SKYACTIV-G (PE-VPS, 165bhp) powered the 2011-2019 Mazda3 (BM/BN), 2012-2020 Mazda6 (GJ/GL), and 2012-2020 CX-5 (KE). Carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection causes rough idle, misfires, and reduced power. Rebuilt units include professionally cleaned intake valves, new piston rings, and a full gasket set.
entries.cta: Get quotes for PE-VPS 2.0L Mazda engine replacement

entries.title: PY-VPS 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: The 2.5L SKYACTIV-G (PY-VPS, 187bhp) featured in the 2012-2020 CX-5 (KE/KF), 2013-2020 Mazda6 (GJ/GL), and 2017-2020 Mazda CX-9 (TC). Carbon buildup, thermostat failure, and occasional cylinder head cracking are the primary replacement drivers. Our rebuilt units include valve cleaning, new thermostat, and a pressure-tested cylinder head.
entries.cta: Compare PY-VPS 2.5L Mazda petrol engine prices

entries.title: 13B-MSP Renesis 1.3L Rotary (Petrol
entries.description: ) The 1.3L Renesis rotary (13B-MSP, 228-238bhp) powered the 2003-2012 Mazda RX-8 (SE3P). Apex and side seal wear cause low compression, hot-start failure, and eventual housing damage — typically between 50,000-80,000 miles. Our rebuilt units include new OEM-spec seals, upgraded bearings, lapped housings, and full bench testing.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 13B-MSP Renesis rotary engine replacement

entries.title: 13B-REW 1.3L Twin-Turbo Rotary (Petrol
entries.description: ) The legendary 1.3L twin-turbo rotary (13B-REW, 255-276bhp) powered the 1992-2002 Mazda RX-7 (FD). Apex seal wear, turbocharger failure, and cooling system issues are common. Our rebuilt units include new apex seals, upgraded bearings, rebuilt turbos, and comprehensive bench testing.

entries.title: BP / BP4W / BP6D 1.8L Petrol
entries.description: The 1.8L BP engine powered the 1998-2005 Mazda MX-5 (NB) and 1994-1998 Mazda 323F (BJ). Timing belt failure (interference engine), hydraulic lifter tick, and cam angle sensor failure are the primary issues. Our rebuilt units include a new timing belt kit, water pump, cam seals, and a full gasket set.
entries.cta: Get quotes for BP 1.8L Mazda engine replacement

entries.title: B6 1.6L Petrol
entries.description: The 1.6L B6 engine powered the 1989-1997 Mazda MX-5 (NA) and 1990-1994 Mazda 323 (BG). Age-related seal degradation, timing belt wear, and head gasket failure are common. Our rebuilt units include new timing belt, water pump, head gasket, and all seals. Closing line (Block A): Can't find your engine code listed above? engineYears.h2: Mazda Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When

years.preview: Pre-Skyactiv Era: RF Diesel, KL V6 & BP Petrol Family
years.description: Mazda's late 1990s UK range was powered by a mix of Ford-derived and in-house engines across the 323, 626, MX-5, MPV, and Premacy, establishing a broad but complex engine portfolio before the brand's major technological transition.
keyChanges: RF 2.0-litre diesel (indirect injection) continues across 626 and Premacy - timing belt-driven interference engine requiring strict service intervals
KL 2.5-litre V6 DOHC petrol introduced to UK in 626 and MPV variants - one of Mazda's smoothest naturally aspirated units of the era
BP 1.8-litre and FS 2.0-litre four-cylinder DOHC petrols are the dominant engines across 323 and 626
MX-5 NB generation arrives in UK with 1.6 B6 and 1.8 BP-ZE - MX-5 becomes the defining Mazda driver's car
FS-DE 2.0 petrol introduced in the 626 and Premacy as the principal family petrol
mainEngines: RF-CX / RF2A - 2.0 indirect diesel (626, Premacy, 323F)
KL - 2.5 V6 DOHC petrol (626 V6, MPV)
BP-ZE - 1.8 DOHC petrol (MX-5 NB, 323F)
B6 - 1.6 SOHC petrol (MX-5 NB 1.6, 323 1.6)
FS-DE - 2.0 DOHC petrol (626 2.0, Premacy)
ZM - 1.8 DOHC petrol (323, 323F)
knownFor: RF diesel is an interference engine - timing belt snap destroys the cylinder head; 80,000 km belt change intervals are mandatory and frequently missed on high-mileage UK examples
RF injector flame seals become porous above 120,000 km - diesel seeps into engine oil causing sharp oil consumption rise; this is a documented production fault
BP-ZE 1.8 in the MX-5 is one of the most beloved naturally aspirated sports car engines in the UK market - high-revving, reliable, and affordable to rebuild
KL V6 is smooth and characterful but cam seal oil leaks are common above 100,000 miles - inexpensive to fix but ignored repairs lead to significant oil loss
years.cta: Search for 1998–2002 Mazda replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: Mazda3 and Mazda6 Launch, L-Family & MZR Engine Introduction Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The arrival of the all-new Mazda3 and Mazda6 brought the MZR engine family to UK buyers - a clean-sheet aluminium-block petrol architecture that replaced the older B/BP/FS generation and became the foundation for every Mazda petrol unit until Skyactiv.
keyChanges: Mazda3 (BK) launched in UK with 1.4 Z4, 1.6 Z6, and 2.0 LF MZR petrol engines - a new generation architecture
Mazda6 (GG) arrives with 1.8 L8, 2.0 LF, and 2.3 L3 MZR petrol; 2.0 RF turbo diesel carried over
MZR 2.3-litre L3 turbocharged unit (L3-VDT) introduced in Mazdaspeed/MPS variants - Mazda3 MPS and Mazda6 MPS
RF5C 2.0 turbo diesel updated and widely deployed across Mazda3 and Mazda6 - indirect injection gives way to RF5C common rail variant
MX-5 NB retired; NB to NC transition begins, MX-5 NC arrives with 2.0 LF and 1.8 L8 petrol engines from 2005
mainEngines: LF-DE / LF-VE - 2.0 DOHC MZR petrol (Mazda3, Mazda6, MX-5 NC 2.0)
L8 - 1.8 DOHC MZR petrol (Mazda6, MX-5 NC 1.8, Mazda3 1.8)
L3-VE - 2.3 DOHC MZR petrol (Mazda6, Mazda3)
L3-VDT - 2.3 turbocharged DOHC (Mazda3 MPS, Mazda6 MPS)
RF5C - 2.0 common-rail diesel (Mazda3 2.0 CD, Mazda6 2.0 CD)
Z6 - 1.6 DOHC MZR petrol (Mazda3 1.6)
knownFor: LF-DE and L8 MZR engines are excellent everyday units - highly reliable, responsive, and inexpensive to maintain; cam and crank sensor failures are the primary fault above 100,000 miles
L3-VDT turbocharged MPS unit is energetic and well-regarded by enthusiasts but intercooler boost pipe splits are common on high-mileage or modified examples
RF5C common-rail diesel is a step forward over the RF2A but timing chain (replacing belt on some variants) and EGR fouling remain known fault areas
MX-5 NC with LF 2.0 becomes a modern classic - engine reliability is exceptional and the platform has generated a thriving UK specialist rebuilder community
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Mazda3 2.0 engine replacement UK - Mazda6 2.0 petrol reconditioned engine - Mazda MPS engine for sale UK - Mazda3 1.6 engine supply fit - Mazda6 2.0 diesel engine replacement
years.cta: Compare 2003–2007 Mazda3 and Mazda6 MZR replacement engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: Mazda6 Second Gen, CX-7 Turbo & 2.2 MZR-CD Diesel Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The second-generation Mazda6 introduced the all-new 2.2-litre MZR-CD diesel - the most advanced diesel Mazda had ever offered in the UK - while the CX-7 brought the turbocharged L3 petrol to an SUV bodystyle for the first time.
keyChanges: 2.2 MZR-CD diesel (R2 engine family) introduced in Mazda6 (GH) and CX-7 - common-rail, twin-scroll turbo, 129 PS and 163 PS outputs
CX-7 launched in UK with 2.3-litre L3-VDT turbocharged petrol - direct import of the MPS unit in SUV form
Mazda3 updated (BL generation) - LF and Z6 MZR petrols carried over with refinement improvements
1.6 MZR-CD diesel (Y6 engine) expanded to Mazda3 and Mazda2 - compact four-cylinder common-rail for the smaller model range
Euro 4 compliance across all diesel variants
mainEngines: R2 (SHY1 / SHY3) - 2.2 MZR-CD diesel (Mazda6 GH, CX-7 diesel, CX-9 diesel)
Y6 - 1.6 MZR-CD diesel (Mazda3 1.6 CD, Mazda2 1.6 MZD)
L3-VDT - 2.3 turbo petrol (CX-7, Mazda3 MPS, Mazda6 MPS)
LF-VE - 2.0 MZR petrol (Mazda3 BL, Mazda6, MX-5)
L3-VE - 2.3 MZR petrol (Mazda6, Mazda CX-9)
knownFor: 2.2 MZR-CD diesel is punchy and refined - but early examples suffer DPF blockage on short-journey UK use and a known camshaft adjuster wear fault on high-mileage units
1.6 MZR-CD diesel in Mazda3 is fuel-efficient and compact but DPF and EGR fouling are recurring issues - urban use accelerates failure rates significantly
CX-7 with L3-VDT is an entertaining SUV but high fuel consumption and turbocharger oil feed pipe blockage above 80,000 miles are well-documented
Mazda3 BL with 2.0 MZR petrol is consistently regarded as one of the most reliable family hatchbacks of its generation in the UK market - engine-related failure is uncommon below 150,000 miles
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Mazda6 2.2 diesel engine replacement UK - Mazda 2.2 MZR-CD reconditioned engine - Mazda3 1.6 diesel engine for sale - Mazda CX-7 engine supply UK - Mazda6 GH diesel engine rebuild
years.cta: Find 2007–2011 Mazda6 and CX-7 replacement engines from UK diesel engine specialists

years.preview: Skyactiv-G & Skyactiv-D Launch: A New Architecture Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: 2012 marked the most significant engineering transition in Mazda's modern history - the Skyactiv-G high-compression petrol and Skyactiv-D ultra-low compression diesel replaced the entire MZR family across the new Mazda3, Mazda6, and CX-5, transforming the brand's UK engine landscape in a single model cycle.
keyChanges: Skyactiv-G 2.0 (PE) and 2.5 (PY) petrol engines introduced - 14:1 compression ratio, direct injection, 40% fewer internal components than MZR predecessor
Skyactiv-D 2.2 (SHY) diesel introduced - 14:1 compression ratio (lowest of any production diesel), twin-scroll turbo, no DPF required at launch (Euro 5 compliant)
All-new Mazda6 (GJ), Mazda3 (BM), and CX-5 (KE) launched on Skyactiv platform simultaneously - the brand's biggest single-year product transformation

Early Skyactiv-D suffers critical oil dilution fault - diesel contamination via injector seals causes oil level rise and potential engine failure; Mazda issues worldwide software fix and revised dipstick
Skyactiv chassis, gearbox, and body architecture introduced alongside engine changes
mainEngines: PE-VPS / PE-VPR - 2.0 Skyactiv-G petrol (Mazda3, Mazda6, CX-5, MX-5 ND from 2015)
PY-VPS - 2.5 Skyactiv-G petrol (Mazda6 2.5, CX-5 2.5, Mazda5)
SHY1 / SHY3 - 2.2 Skyactiv-D diesel (Mazda6, Mazda3, CX-5 - 150 PS and 175 PS variants)
P5 - 1.5 Skyactiv-G petrol (Mazda2, Mazda3 1.5 from 2013)
knownFor: Early Skyactiv-D 2.2 diesel (2012–2014) is the highest-risk engine in Mazda's modern UK history - injector seal failures cause diesel to contaminate engine oil, resulting in oil pickup blockage and catastrophic engine failure without warning

Skyactiv-D 2012–2014 turbine diversion valve failures require complete twin-turbo replacement - a significant cost that has driven many UK owners to seek remanufactured engines rather than component repair

Skyactiv-G 2.0 petrol is an outstanding unit - direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves is a known nuisance above 60,000–80,000 miles but does not affect engine longevity when the PCV valve is serviced

Mazda6 GJ and CX-5 KE are well-regarded UK fleet and family cars - petrol variants have achieved 150,000+ miles routinely; early diesel variants are the dominant source of engine replacement enquiries from this period
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Mazda 2.2 Skyactiv-D engine replacement UK - Mazda CX-5 diesel engine oil contamination - Mazda6 Skyactiv-D reconditioned engine - Mazda3 2.0 Skyactiv-G engine for sale - Mazda CX-5 2.2 diesel engine supply fit UK
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012–2014 Mazda Skyactiv-G and Skyactiv-D replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: Skyactiv Maturity, MX-5 ND & Revised 2.2 Diesel Major Reliability Revision
years.description: Mazda addressed the Skyactiv-D diesel's critical early failings with revised injector seals and updated DPF calibration, while the all-new MX-5 ND introduced the lightest iteration of the Skyactiv-G petrol platform and the 2.5-litre turbocharged PY-VPTS arrived in the CX-9.
keyChanges: Skyactiv-D 2.2 revised from 2015 - injector clamp force corrected, oil dilution fault addressed, DPF calibration updated for Euro 6
MX-5 ND launched with 1.5 (P5) and 2.0 (PE) Skyactiv-G - lightest MX-5 in 25 years
2.5-litre turbocharged Skyactiv-G (PY-VPTS) introduced in Mazda CX-9 and later CX-5 upper variants - 227 PS
Mazda6 facelift introduces revised 2.5 Skyactiv-G across saloon and estate for UK market
Euro 6 diesel compliance - revised SHY Skyactiv-D with SCR and AdBlue on higher-output variants
mainEngines: PE-VPS Euro 6 - 2.0 Skyactiv-G petrol (Mazda3, Mazda6, CX-5, MX-5 ND 2.0)
P5-VPS - 1.5 Skyactiv-G petrol (MX-5 ND 1.5, Mazda2)
PY-VPS - 2.5 Skyactiv-G petrol (Mazda6 2.5, CX-5 2.5)
PY-VPTS - 2.5 turbo Skyactiv-G petrol (Mazda CX-9, CX-5 turbo)
SHY3 / SHY6 Euro 6 revised - 2.2 Skyactiv-D diesel (Mazda3, Mazda6, CX-5)
knownFor: Post-2015 Skyactiv-D is dramatically more reliable than 2012–2014 units - specialist consensus is that the injector fix genuinely solved the core fault, though oil dilution risk from short-journey driving remains relevant
MAF sensor contamination on Skyactiv-G 2.0 and 2.5 is a well-documented pattern fault - failure typically triggers P0101 or P0171 codes and is commonly required between 60,000–80,000 miles

MX-5 ND 1.5 Skyactiv-G is celebrated as one of the finest small-displacement naturally aspirated sports car engines in contemporary production - low failure rate; cam chain tensioner wear is a very minor watchpoint on early cars

2.5 turbocharged CX-9 and CX-5 unit is strong but coolant leak from the water jacket is subject to ongoing class-action activity in the US market - UK examples should have cooling system inspected at service intervals above 60,000 miles
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Mazda CX-5 2.2 diesel reconditioned engine UK - Mazda6 2.2 Skyactiv-D engine for sale - Mazda MX-5 ND engine replacement UK - Mazda3 2.0 Skyactiv engine supply fit - Mazda 2.5 turbo engine replacement UK
years.cta: Find 2015–2018 Mazda Skyactiv-G and revised Skyactiv-D replacement engines from UK rebuilders

years.preview: Skyactiv-X SPCCI Launch & CX-30 Introduction Technology Introduction
years.description: Mazda launched the world's first production petrol engine using spark-controlled compression ignition (SPCCI) - the Skyactiv-X - in the all-new fourth-generation Mazda3 and CX-30, representing the most technically ambitious combustion engine development programme in the brand's history.
keyChanges: Skyactiv-X 2.0 SPCCI engine (PE-VXPS) launched in Mazda3 (BP) and CX-30 - combines petrol and diesel combustion modes in a single unit
CX-30 introduced as a new crossover model - positioned between Mazda2 and CX-5 in UK range
Skyactiv-D diesel discontinued from new Mazda3 - petrol-only strategy for the new generation compact car
2.0 Skyactiv-G (PE) continues in Mazda3 and CX-30 as the accessible petrol alternative to Skyactiv-X
Skyactiv-X does not reach US market - UK and European markets are the primary Skyactiv-X sales territories
mainEngines: PE-VXPS - 2.0 Skyactiv-X SPCCI petrol 178 PS (Mazda3 BP, CX-30 - from 2019)
PE-VPS - 2.0 Skyactiv-G petrol 122 PS (Mazda3, CX-30 entry, CX-5)
SHY3 / SHY6 - 2.2 Skyactiv-D diesel (CX-5, Mazda6 continued)
PY-VPTS - 2.5 turbo Skyactiv-G (CX-5 Turbo, CX-9)
knownFor: Skyactiv-X SPCCI is a genuinely groundbreaking engineering achievement - SPCCI compression ignition mode delivers improved torque response and fuel efficiency over Skyactiv-G, but only activates under specific load and temperature conditions
Real-world fuel economy of Skyactiv-X falls short of official figures for most UK drivers - the SPCCI mode engages less frequently in urban and stop-start conditions than Mazda's testing data implies

Skyactiv-X OPF (Otto particle filter, equivalent to GPF) fitted to EU market cars adds a potential failure point at high mileage - similar maintenance implications to a diesel DPF on petrol-fuelled regeneration cycles

Early 2019 Mazda3 BP had minor quality control faults (seals, trim) but the Skyactiv-X engine itself has accumulated a reasonable reliability record to date - no catastrophic failure patterns reported in the UK specialist community
years.cta: Compare 2019–2020 Mazda Skyactiv-X and Skyactiv-G replacement engine quotes from UK specialists

years.preview: e-Skyactiv MHEV, Inline-Six Arrival & MX-30 Electric Technology Introduction
years.description: Mazda wrapped the Skyactiv-X in mild-hybrid technology to create the e-Skyactiv-X, introduced 24V M Hybrid mild-hybrid to the Skyactiv-G petrol range across the UK lineup, and launched the inline-six turbocharged Skyactiv-G 3.3 in the all-new CX-60 and CX-80 as the brand's new premium platform.
keyChanges: e-Skyactiv-X updated from 2021 - output rises to 183 PS, MHEV 24V belt-integrated starter-generator added, CO₂ drops 5–11 g/km
e-Skyactiv-G MHEV 24V (M Hybrid) fitted to Mazda2, Mazda3, CX-30, and CX-5 petrol variants from 2022
Skyactiv-G 3.3 inline-six turbocharged (S8-DPTS) introduced in CX-60 (2022) - Mazda's first inline-six since the 1990s
CX-60 also offers plug-in hybrid (e-Skyactiv PHEV) combining 2.5 petrol with 100 kW electric motor
MX-30 battery electric launched in UK - single 143 PS electric motor, 35.5 kWh battery
Mazda6 officially discontinued (2021) - no direct replacement confirmed for UK
mainEngines: PE-VXPS (e-Skyactiv-X) - 2.0 SPCCI MHEV 183 PS (Mazda3, CX-30 - from 2021)
PE-VPS (e-Skyactiv-G) - 2.0 MHEV petrol 122 PS (Mazda3, CX-30, CX-5 - MHEV spec)
PY-VPS (e-Skyactiv-G) - 2.5 MHEV petrol 194 PS (CX-5 upper range - from 2023)
S8-DPTS - 3.3 inline-six turbo petrol 280 PS (CX-60, CX-80)
e-Skyactiv PHEV - 2.5 petrol + 100 kW motor (CX-60 PHEV)
MX-30 electric motor - 143 PS single motor (MX-30 EV)
popularModels: Mazda CX-30 (DM - e-Skyactiv G and X)
knownFor: e-Skyactiv-X delivers meaningful real-world improvement over the pre-MHEV version - the MHEV belt-ISG smooths low-speed driving considerably and reduces cold-start emissions

CX-60 3.3 inline-six is one of the most driver-focused engines Mazda has offered in decades - praised for refinement and torque delivery, but early CX-60 UK production encountered suspension calibration and software issues unrelated to the engine itself
e-Skyactiv-G MHEV in CX-5 is well-received for everyday use - cylinder deactivation on 165 PS auto variants reduces fuel consumption meaningfully without compromising response
CX-60 PHEV high-voltage battery thermal management and charging reliability are emerging ownership watchpoints - independent repair costs for PHEV drivetrain failures outside warranty are significant
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Mazda CX-5 e-Skyactiv engine replacement UK - Mazda3 2.0 MHEV engine for sale - Mazda CX-60 3.3 engine supply UK - Mazda e-Skyactiv-X reconditioned engine - Mazda CX-30 engine replacement UK specialist
years.cta: Get quotes for 2021–2025 Mazda e-Skyactiv and inline-six replacement engines from verified UK specialists

faq.h2: Mazda Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Mazda owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Mazda engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A typical Mazda engine replacement costs between £1,800 and £4,500 for supply and fit, depending on the model and engine code. A rebuilt 1.6-litre MZR petrol for a Mazda3 might start around £1,800, while a 2.2-litre Skyactiv-D for a CX-5 typically lands between £3,200 and £4,500. Reconditioned engines with upgraded internals (e.g., revised injector seals, updated timing chains) cost 20–30% more than basic used units but include a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Always compare supply‑only versus supply‑and‑fit – fitting adds £400–£800 depending on the specialist.
items.cta: Compare Mazda engine replacement prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a Mazda engine replacement?
items.answer: Four main factors: engine size and type (2.2‑litre Skyactiv‑D costs far more than 1.5‑litre petrol), condition (rebuilt with warranty vs used as‑is), vehicle complexity (Mazda CX‑5 takes longer than a Mazda3), and supply‑only vs supply‑and‑fit. Location also matters – labour rates in London and the South East can be 20–30% higher than the Midlands or North. The cheapest option is often a used engine without warranty, but a rebuilt unit with upgraded parts typically offers better long‑term value, especially for Skyactiv‑D models prone to carbon coking. Our comparison tool brings transparent pricing from multiple UK specialists.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Mazda engine specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Mazda?
items.answer: In most cases, yes – especially if the chassis, gearbox, and bodywork are in good condition. Rebuilt Mazda engines often incorporate upgraded components that address the original failure points (e.g., redesigned EGR coolers on Skyactiv‑D, beefed‑up timing chain tensioners on MZR petrols), making them more durable than the factory original. The alternative – selling the car as a non‑runner and buying another used vehicle – typically costs more than an engine replacement, particularly for popular models like the Mazda3, Mazda6, or CX-5 that hold their value well. If your Mazda is worth more than £2,500 and is otherwise solid, replacement is almost always the financially sensible route.
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to see whether a rebuilt or used Mazda engine makes financial sense

items.question: What's included in a Mazda engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full supply‑and‑fit service includes sourcing the correct engine (by your specific engine code), removing the failed unit, installing the replacement with new fluids, filters, and gaskets, a full system test‑drive, and disposal of your old engine. Most specialists also include a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty on the replacement engine, covering major internal components. Labour for fitting typically adds £400–£800, but this varies by model – a straightforward Mazda3 MZR swap is on the lower end, while a CX‑5 Skyactiv‑D with complex emissions equipment is higher. Always confirm what's included – some suppliers charge extra for coolant, oil, or delivery.
items.cta: Compare Mazda engine supply and fit deals

items.question: How long does a Mazda engine replacement take?
items.answer: For most common models – Mazda3, Mazda6, CX‑5 – count on 3 to 5 working days from booking to driving away. More complex or less common engines (e.g., RX‑8 Renesis rotary, Mazda3 MPS turbo) may take 5 to 7 days because of specialist tooling or lower parts availability. The single biggest variable is sourcing the correct engine code – our network of UK specialists stocks commonly replaced units like the LF17 MZR petrol and SH Skyactiv‑D diesel, which keeps lead times short. Ask your chosen supplier for a firm timeline when you get your quote, and factor in any delivery delays if you're supplying the engine yourself.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Mazda engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: Do reconditioned Mazda engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes – every reconditioned or rebuilt Mazda engine supplied through EngineMarket comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Many specialists offer extended 24‑month cover on fully remanufactured units, particularly for high‑volume engines like the 2.2‑litre Skyactiv‑D and 2.0‑litre MZR petrol. The warranty typically covers major internal components: crankshaft, connecting rods, pistons, timing chain, oil pump, and cylinder head. It does not cover ancillary parts you supply yourself (e.g., turbo, injectors) or damage from external causes. We always recommend reading the exact terms from your chosen supplier – but a 12‑month warranty is the industry standard for reconditioned Mazda engines.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Mazda engine specialists

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Mazda engine?
items.answer: Used engines are cheaper upfront – typically £800–£1,500 less than a reconditioned unit – and make sense as a temporary fix, for a car you plan to sell within a year, or for models with very low failure rates (e.g., Mazda2 1.5 petrol). Reconditioned engines cost more but include new rings, bearings, seals, and gaskets, plus a 12‑24 month warranty. For failure‑prone Mazda families – particularly the 2.2‑litre Skyactiv‑D (carbon coking) and MZR 2.0/2.3 petrols (timing chain stretch) – a reconditioned unit with upgraded components is strongly recommended. It will likely outlast a used engine and give you genuine peace of mind.
items.cta: Compare used vs recon Mazda engine prices on EngineMarket

items.question: Where can I find Mazda engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK‑wide network of vetted Mazda engine specialists – from independent rebuilders in the Midlands to South East supply‑and‑fit centres. There are no exclusive regional clusters for Mazda (unlike Land Rover in the Peak District), but our registration lookup tool instantly matches you with nearby suppliers who stock your specific engine code and offer fitting. Nationwide delivery is available for supply‑only buyers, and many specialists offer mobile fitting or will arrange collection of your vehicle. Enter your reg to see the best‑rated Mazda engine specialists in your area.
items.cta: Find Mazda engine specialists near you with our free comparison tool

items.question: What are the most common Mazda engine failures?
items.answer: The four most common failures driving Mazda engine replacement in the UK: 1) Skyactiv‑D 2.2 carbon coking – severe intake buildup causing P2002 codes, DPF failure, and eventual engine death (CX‑5, Mazda6, 2012–2018). 2) MZR‑CD diesel injector leakage – fuel dilution destroys bottom ends on R2AA/R2BA engines (Mazda6, Mazda3, 2005–2012). 3) MZR petrol timing chain stretch – chain jumps, bends valves on LF17, L813, L3‑VE (Mazda3, Mazda6, MX‑5 NC, 2004–2013). 4) Skyactiv‑G direct‑injection carbon buildup – intake valve coking causes cold misfires (Mazda3, CX‑5, MX‑5 ND, 2012+). All four failures typically lead to replacement rather than repair once symptomatic.
items.cta: Compare prices for rebuilt Mazda engines with upgraded components

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Mazda engine?
items.answer: Compare at least three quotes – prices for the same engine code can vary by £1,000+ between suppliers. Consider a quality used engine if your budget is tight and the car has low value; choose a reconditioned unit with upgraded parts if you plan to keep the car for 3+ years. Ask whether the quoted price includes delivery, fitting, new fluids, and VAT – many "cheap" quotes exclude one or more of these. If you have a trusted local garage, ask for a supply‑only quote and arrange fitting separately (you may save £200–£400). Finally, check warranty terms – a slightly more expensive engine with 24‑month cover can be better value than a cheaper 12‑month unit.
